title: lesquerella kingii subsp. diversifolia
intro: Lesquerella kingii subsp. diversifolia, also known as Diverse Bladderpod, is a subspecies of annual plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, which have been attributed to its high content of sulfur and other compounds. The health benefits of Diverse Bladderpod include its ability to soothe and heal skin irritations, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion or decoction, and has been used in the form of salves, ointments, and teas.
